speaker: BRUTUS
text: In this point charge him home, that he affects

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Tyrannical power. If he evade us there,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Enforce him with his envy to the people,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: And that the spoil got on the Antiates

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Was ne’er distributed.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: What, will he come?

speaker: AEDILE
text: He’s coming.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: How accompanied?

speaker: AEDILE
text: With old Menenius, and those senators

speaker: AEDILE
text: That always favored him.

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Have you a catalogue

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Of all the voices that we have procured,

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Set down by th’ poll?

speaker: AEDILE
text: I have. ’Tis ready.

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Have you collected them by tribes?

speaker: AEDILE
text: I have.

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Assemble presently the people hither;

speaker: SICINIUS
text: And when they hear me say “It shall be so

speaker: SICINIUS
text: I’ th’ right and strength o’ th’ commons,” be it either

speaker: SICINIUS
text: For death, for fine, or banishment, then let them

speaker: SICINIUS
text: If I say “Fine,” cry “Fine,” if “Death,” cry “Death,”

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Insisting on the old prerogative

speaker: SICINIUS
text: And power i’ th’ truth o’ th’ cause.

speaker: AEDILE
text: I shall inform them.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: And when such time they have begun to cry,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Let them not cease, but with a din confused

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Enforce the present execution

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Of what we chance to sentence.

speaker: AEDILE
text: Very well.

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Make them be strong and ready for this hint

speaker: SICINIUS
text: When we shall hap to give ’t them.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Go about it.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Put him to choler straight. He hath been used

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Ever to conquer and to have his worth

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Of contradiction. Being once chafed, he cannot

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Be reined again to temperance; then he speaks

speaker: BRUTUS
text: What’s in his heart, and that is there which looks

speaker: BRUTUS
text: With us to break his neck.

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Well, here he comes.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Calmly, I do beseech you.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Ay, as an hostler that for th’ poorest piece

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Will bear the knave by th’ volume.—Th’ honored gods

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Keep Rome in safety and the chairs of justice

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Supplied with worthy men! Plant love among ’s!

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Throng our large temples with the shows of peace

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: And not our streets with war!

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: Amen, amen.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: A noble wish.

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Draw near, you people.

speaker: AEDILE
text: List to your tribunes. Audience! Peace, I say!

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: First, hear me speak.

speaker:
BOTH
               
              TRIBUNES
text: Well, say.—Peace, ho!

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Shall I be charged no further than this present?

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Must all determine here?

speaker: SICINIUS
text: I do demand

speaker: SICINIUS
text: If you submit you to the people’s voices,

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Allow their officers, and are content

speaker: SICINIUS
text: To suffer lawful censure for such faults

speaker: SICINIUS
text: As shall be proved upon you.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: I am content.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Lo, citizens, he says he is content.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: The warlike service he has done, consider. Think

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Upon the wounds his body bears, which show

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Like graves i’ th’ holy churchyard.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Scratches with briars,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Scars to move laughter only.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Consider further,

speaker: MENENIUS
text: That when he speaks not like a citizen,

speaker: MENENIUS
text: You find him like a soldier. Do not take

speaker: MENENIUS
text: His rougher accents for malicious sounds,

speaker: MENENIUS
text: But, as I say, such as become a soldier

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Rather than envy you.

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Well, well, no more.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: What is the matter,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: That, being passed for consul with full voice,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: I am so dishonored that the very hour

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: You take it off again?

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Answer to us.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Say then. ’Tis true, I ought so.

speaker: SICINIUS
text: We charge you that you have contrived to take

speaker: SICINIUS
text: From Rome all seasoned office and to wind

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Yourself into a power tyrannical,

speaker: SICINIUS
text: For which you are a traitor to the people.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: How? Traitor?

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Nay, temperately! Your promise.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: The fires i’ th’ lowest hell fold in the people!

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Call me their traitor? Thou injurious tribune!

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Within thine eyes sat twenty thousand deaths,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: In thy hands clutched as many millions, in

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Thy lying tongue both numbers, I would say

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: “Thou liest” unto thee with a voice as free

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: As I do pray the gods.

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Mark you this, people?

speaker:
ALL
               
              PLEBEIANS
text: To th’ rock, to th’ rock with him!

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Peace!

speaker: SICINIUS
text: We need not put new matter to his charge.

speaker: SICINIUS
text: What you have seen him do and heard him speak,

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Beating your officers, cursing yourselves,

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Opposing laws with strokes, and here defying

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Those whose great power must try him—even this,

speaker: SICINIUS
text: So criminal and in such capital kind,

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Deserves th’ extremest death.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: But since he hath

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Served well for Rome—

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: What do you prate of service?

speaker: BRUTUS
text: I talk of that that know it.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: You?

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Is this the promise that you made your mother?

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Know, I pray you—

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: I’ll know no further.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Let them pronounce the steep Tarpeian death,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Vagabond exile, flaying, pent to linger

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: But with a grain a day, I would not buy

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Their mercy at the price of one fair word,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Nor check my courage for what they can give,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: To have ’t with saying “Good morrow.”

speaker: SICINIUS
text: For that he has,

speaker: SICINIUS
text: As much as in him lies, from time to time

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Envied against the people, seeking means

speaker: SICINIUS
text: To pluck away their power, as now at last

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Given hostile strokes, and that not in the presence

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Of dreaded justice, but on the ministers

speaker: SICINIUS
text: That doth distribute it, in the name o’ th’ people

speaker: SICINIUS
text: And in the power of us the Tribunes, we,

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Even from this instant, banish him our city

speaker: SICINIUS
text: In peril of precipitation

speaker: SICINIUS
text: From off the rock Tarpeian, never more

speaker: SICINIUS
text: To enter our Rome gates. I’ th’ people’s name,

speaker: SICINIUS
text: I say it shall be so.

speaker:
ALL
               
              PLEBEIANS
text: It shall be so, it shall be so! Let him away!

speaker:
ALL
               
              PLEBEIANS
text: He’s banished, and it shall be so.

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Hear me, my masters and my common friends—

speaker: SICINIUS
text: He’s sentenced. No more hearing.

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Let me speak.

speaker: COMINIUS
text: I have been consul and can show for Rome

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Her enemies’ marks upon me. I do love

speaker: COMINIUS
text: My country’s good with a respect more tender,

speaker: COMINIUS
text: More holy and profound, than mine own life,

speaker: COMINIUS
text: My dear wife’s estimate, her womb’s increase,

speaker: COMINIUS
text: And treasure of my loins. Then if I would

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Speak that—

speaker: SICINIUS
text: We know your drift. Speak what?

speaker: BRUTUS
text: There’s no more to be said, but he is banished

speaker: BRUTUS
text: As enemy to the people and his country.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: It shall be so.

speaker:
ALL
               
              PLEBEIANS
text: It shall be so, it shall be so!

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: You common cry of curs, whose breath I hate

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: As reek o’ th’ rotten fens, whose loves I prize

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: As the dead carcasses of unburied men

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: That do corrupt my air, I banish you!

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: And here remain with your uncertainty;

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Let every feeble rumor shake your hearts;

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Your enemies, with nodding of their plumes,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Fan you into despair! Have the power still

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: To banish your defenders, till at length

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Your ignorance—which finds not till it feels,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Making but reservation of yourselves,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Still your own foes—deliver you

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: As most abated captives to some nation

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: That won you without blows! Despising

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: For you the city, thus I turn my back.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: There is a world elsewhere.

speaker: AEDILE
text: The people’s enemy is gone, is gone.

speaker:
ALL
               
              PLEBEIANS
text: Our enemy is banished; he is gone. Hoo, hoo!

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Go see him out at gates, and follow him,

speaker: SICINIUS
text: As he hath followed you, with all despite.

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Give him deserved vexation. Let a guard

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Attend us through the city.

speaker:
ALL
               
              PLEBEIANS
text: Come, come, let’s see him out at gates! Come!

speaker:
ALL
               
              PLEBEIANS
text: The gods preserve our noble tribunes! Come!
